summary: End-to-end curriculum design process from needs analysis through implementation, using backward design and standards alignment.

inputs.name: learner_population
inputs.description: Target learner demographics, grade level, and prior knowledge.

inputs.name: standards_or_competencies
inputs.description: Applicable education standards, competency frameworks, or learning goals.

inputs.name: design_constraints
inputs.description: Time, resource, technology, or institutional constraints.

outputs.name: curriculum_framework
outputs.description: Complete curriculum structure with units, lessons, and learning progressions.

outputs.name: alignment_matrix
outputs.description: Mapping of standards to curriculum units and assessments.

outputs.name: implementation_guide
outputs.description: Educator-facing guide for delivering the curriculum.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is curriculum design, not single lesson planning or assessment-only work.
Require learner population and standards before design begins.
Output must include curriculum framework, alignment matrix, and implementation guide.

steps.action: Conduct needs analysis for the target learner population and context.
Map applicable standards to curriculum units and learning progressions.
Design instructional strategies and learning experiences for each unit.
Design assessments aligned to objectives and create alignment matrix.
Create implementation guide with pacing, materials, and educator notes.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read standards documents, existing curriculum, and learner data.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Save curriculum framework, alignment matrix, and implementation guide.

verification.method: Check that curriculum framework covers all units and lessons.
Check that every standard maps to at least one unit and assessment.
Check that implementation guide is specific enough for an educator to follow.

success_criteria: Curriculum framework covers all required standards.
Alignment matrix maps every standard to curriculum and assessment.
Implementation guide is actionable for the target educator audience.

rollback.when: If the curriculum invents standards or misrepresents learner needs, discard and restart from verified inputs.
